title: Gigabrain: Memory OS for AI Agents
description: A guided tour through Gigabrain's architecture — from plugin entry point to capture pipelines, recall orchestration, world-model projection, and the Obsidian vault surface.
prerequisites: Familiarity with SQLite and event-sourcing patterns

steps.title: Welcome to Gigabrain
steps.description: Gigabrain is a long-term memory layer for OpenClaw agents. It captures memories from conversations, stores them in an event-sourced SQLite database, projects them into a structured world model, and surfaces them as an Obsidian vault. The codebase is organized into a plugin entry point, a core services library, CLI scripts, tests, and an optional web console.

steps.title: Plugin Entry Point
steps.description: index.ts is the OpenClaw plugin factory. It wires up event hooks (on:prompt-start for recall injection, on:response-complete for memory capture) and registers HTTP routes on the gateway. openclaw.plugin.json defines the full configuration schema — paths, capture settings, recall tuning, world-model options, and vault output.

interactiveActions.description: Click index.ts to see the plugin factory and event hooks

steps.title: Core Services Overview
steps.description: The lib/core/ directory contains all service implementations (~14K lines). Key modules: config.js validates settings, capture-service.js extracts memories, recall-service.js searches and injects context, orchestrator.js selects recall strategies, world-model.js builds entity/belief/episode graphs, projection-store.js manages SQLite state, and vault-mirror.js generates the Obsidian surface.

interactiveActions.description: Hover over lib/core to see all service modules

steps.title: Capture Pipeline
steps.description: When an agent response completes, capture-service.js extracts <memory_note> XML tags and 'remember intent' phrases. Each candidate passes through policy.js — a junk filter catches system prompts, API keys, and benchmarks, while durable patterns preserve user facts and identity notes. Survivors are deduplicated (exact hash + semantic similarity), scored for quality, and upserted into the projection store. native-promotion.js can also promote chunks from MEMORY.md and daily notes into structured registry entries.

interactiveActions.description: Click capture-service.js to see how memories are extracted from agent responses

steps.title: Recall & Orchestration
steps.description: Before each prompt, orchestrator.js classifies the query into one of five strategies: quick_context (FTS5 lexical search), entity_brief ('Who is X?'), relationship_brief, timeline_brief, or verification_lookup. recall-service.js executes the chosen strategy — searching memory_current with entity coreference resolution, temporal window detection, and class budgets (core/situational/decisions). Results are ranked, token-budgeted, and injected as context. llm-router.js abstracts over Ollama, OpenAI-compatible, and deterministic-only providers.

interactiveActions.description: Click orchestrator.js to see the five recall strategies

steps.title: World Model & Persistence
steps.description: world-model.js (the largest module at 2,600 lines) projects atomic memories into structured graphs: entities (person, project, org, place, topic), beliefs (fact-based assertions with temporal markers), episodes (event clusters), open loops (unresolved follow-ups), and syntheses (precomputed briefings). projection-store.js manages the SQLite schema — memory_current provides a materialized view, while event-store.js maintains an append-only log for full audit history. native-sync.js indexes MEMORY.md and daily notes for hybrid native+structured recall.

interactiveActions.description: Click world-model.js to see entity/belief/episode projection

steps.title: The Full Picture
steps.description: Beyond core services: vault-mirror.js generates a structured Obsidian vault surface (Home, Entities, Nodes, Views, Briefings, Reports). maintenance-service.js runs a nightly pipeline (snapshot, sync, dedupe, audit, vacuum, vault build). scripts/ has the CLI control plane (gigabrainctl.js) and setup wizard. tests/ provides 20 tests across unit, integration, regression, and performance categories. memory_api/ is an optional Python FastAPI web console for browsing and managing memories.

interactiveActions.description: Explore the full codebase — click into any area that interests you
